Description: Loss of bond between finish and substrate or between layers (including weak-boundary-layer driven delamination).
Nonconforming surface texture or flow/leveling appearance (runs, orange peel, roughness).
Foreign matter or surface-energy contamination causing defects (particles, fisheyes, embedded objects).
Visual attributes out of spec (color/shade, gloss/reflectivity, mottling/uneven appearance).
Coating/plating/anodize thickness or continuity out of spec (thin, bare/skip, heavy).
Cure/bake/film-formation problems in organic coatings (soft, brittle, wrinkled).
Bubbles, pinholes, porosity, or blistering (solvent pop, osmotic).
Corrosion products or underfilm corrosion mechanisms (red rust, white corrosion, filiform/creep).
Physical damage or base-material integrity issues (scratches, rack marks, cracking).
Keep-out zone protection failures (overspray, masking failure, plugged features).
Electrochemical/mechanical finishing-specific surface conditions (burn/over-etch, pits, swirl/buff lines).
Labeling/traceability/documentation defects affecting product identification and shipment integrity.
Finish lifts/peels to bare substrate (or plating lifts to base), including loss of bond at the substrate interface.
Color not within approved standard/tolerance (wrong paint lot, dye mismatch, shade drift, incorrect mix).
Microblisters or "pops" from trapped volatiles/solvent/water flashing during bake or cure.
Crater/fisheye defects driven by low-surface-energy contamination (silicone/oil/soap) or incompatible residues causing dewetting.
Ferrous corrosion visible (red rust, rust-through, rust staining) indicating protection failure or bare exposure.
Insufficient thickness/coverage versus spec (e.g., light coverage in functional area; underbuild on edges/complex geometry).
Insufficient cure/bake/crosslinking (soft, tacky, poor solvent resistance, poor durability).
Linear scratches, scuffs, abrasion marks, or rub-through damage from handling, conveyance, or rework.
Wrong label information (part number, quantity, customer, revision, lot) or label applied to the wrong container/part.
Unwanted spray deposition outside intended area (overspray haze, blowby, fogging onto adjacent surfaces).
Burning/over-etch appearance from current density/chemistry imbalance (anodize burn, electropolish burn, plating burn).
Wavy, pebbled "orange peel" texture from poor flow/leveling, viscosity/atomization, or film-build/gel effects.
Delamination between coating layers (e.g., e-coat to topcoat, primer to topcoat, clear to base) with intact substrate bond.
Gloss, sheen, or reflectivity nonconformance (too dull/too glossy; includes brightness nonuniformity on polished/plated finishes when specified).
Blistering driven by soluble salts/solutes and moisture migration (osmotic mechanism), often leading to underfilm corrosion risk.
Visible particulate contamination on/in the finish (dust nibs, lint, smut/particulates in specialty finishing) that causes rejects or rework.
White corrosion products on zinc/aluminum/galvanized substrates (white rust/oxide) under or adjacent to finish.
Uncoated/undercoated area (miss, holiday, skip in electrochemical processes, poor electrical contact causing "skip").
Excessive cure/overbake causing brittleness, discoloration/yellowing, loss of flexibility, or premature cracking/chipping.
Contact marks from racking/fixturing/hangers (bare spots, contact scars, clamp marks) visible after processing.
Missing/incorrect traceability or documentation (missing labels, incorrect travelers/pack slips, lot/date/shift data missing).
Paint/finish enters a keep-out zone due to masking failure or misapplication (tape lift, poor seal, wrong mask placement).
Pits/nodules/etch defects specific to electrochemical finishing (acid pits in anodize, electropolish pitting, plating pits/nodules).
Gravity flow defects: runs, sags, curtains, or drip marks (including drain marks on immersion-coated parts).
Localized edge lift, corner pullback with adhesion loss, or small-area delamination typically driven by poor prep, coverage, or boundary layer at edges.
Uneven appearance (mottle, patchiness, metallic orientation issues, striping) visible under normal viewing conditions.
Pinholes or porosity through the film (outgassing, air entrapment, contamination, or inadequate flow-out) that compromises protection/appearance.
Foreign material physically present on the part (embedded in finish or attached) such as metal chips, magnets, fibers, grit, or other inclusions.
Filiform/creep/undercut corrosion mechanisms under organic films (often starting at defects, edges, or scribes).
Thickness exceeds spec (heavy coat, excessive buildup, plating too thick) leading to fit issues, cure/flow defects, or appearance problems.
Wrinkled or "alligator" surface from film-formation imbalance (skin-over, solvent entrapment, incorrect bake profile, incompatible recoats).
Base material cracking/fracture (e.g., flange cracking) identified during or after finishing; not caused by coating chemistry.
Blocked holes/threads/features due to coating buildup or plug failure (functional nonconformance).
Visible swirl/buff lines or directional finishing marks on mechanically finished surfaces outside visual standard.
Rough, sandy, or powdery texture from dry spray, poor melt/flow, low atomization energy, or insufficient wetting/leveling.
